title: Northline Goods — fictional homepage reveal
disclaimer: Northline Goods is invented. This demonstrates the report structure and does not claim a real audit, outcome, customer, or compliance result.
executiveSummary: The sample homepage presents three equal-weight decisions before one clear offer and its supporting proof. The recommended direction reduces that competition while preserving exploration.

findings.title: Primary action competes with navigation
findings.summary: Three equal-weight actions arrive before supporting proof.
findings.evidence: Static hierarchy review at desktop and mobile widths.
findings.recommendedFix: Use one dominant action and demote supporting links.

findings.title: Muted context needs contrast verification
findings.summary: Supporting copy may be difficult to distinguish from the surface.
findings.evidence: Sample computed-color reference requires final browser measurement.
findings.standardReference: WCAG 2.2 contrast criteria for final verification
findings.recommendedFix: Raise foreground contrast and measure all states.

findings.title: Trust arrives after the first decision
findings.summary: Shipping and material context appears below the initial purchase choice.
findings.recommendedFix: Bring one concise reassurance beside the first action.

experiments.hypothesis: A single reassurance beside the primary action may reduce decision uncertainty.
experiments.change: Add one factual shipping boundary beside the action.

roadmap.work: Measure contrast and confirm business priority.
Implement offer, proof, and action hierarchy.
